case_title: STEVE RYAN VS. IAN KELLEY ET AL

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF EDWARD HIGGINBOTHAM (TRANSACTION ID # 16063037) FILED BY DEFENDANT KELLEY, IAN
POS. DISCO PERSONAL DELIVERY (TRANSACTION ID # 16101038) FILED BY DEFENDANT KELLEY, IAN

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT IAN KELLEY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION FOR LEGAL MALPRACTICE AND UNJUST ENRICHMENT AND OVERRULED AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD. SEE MINUTES AND/OR TRANSCRIPT FOR DETAILS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.
PROOF OF SERVICE OF REPLY TO OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 59328609) FILED BY DEFENDANT KELLEY, IAN
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-02-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT IAN KELLEY'S DEMURRER TO OCUNTS ONE, TWO, AND FIVE OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP 430.41. THE STATUTE REQUIRES COUNSEL TO MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE. THE TIME FOR FILING A DEMURRER IS HEREBY EXTENDED TO SEPTEMBER 15, 2016. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR #11332, BUS. TEL: 415-551-3745. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT IAN KELLEY'S SECOND MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES; AND FOR TERMINATING AND MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1873.00 IS TRANSFERRED TO BE HEARD AT 9:00 A.M. ON THE COURT'S DISCOVERY CALENDAR IN ROOM 301 ON THE SAME DATE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR JAN-24-2017 IN DEPARTMENT 610 AT 10:30 AM FOR FAILURE TO OBTAIN AN ANSWER(S) FROM, OR ENTER DEFAULT(S) AGAINST, DEFENDANT(S) AS TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEC-07-2016 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR (TRANSACTION ID # 16178029) FILED BY DEFENDANT KELLEY, IAN

DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE RESPONSES SERVED BY PLAINTIFF RYAN ON MAY 31 WERE DEVOID OF SUBSTANCE, AND THE BLANKET OBJECTIONS INCLUDED IN THOSE RESPONSES WERE FRIVOLOUS AND IMPROPER, AND ARE HENCE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE OBJECTION-FREE RESPONSES TO ALL DISCOVERY REQUESTS THAT WERE THE SUBJECT OF THIS MOTION WITHIN 20 DAYS OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER. THE OPPOSITION BRIEF WAS FILED ON JUNE 28, WHICH IS LATE UNDER CCP SEC. 1005 (NINE COURT DAYS REQUIRED). THE COURT MAY IGNORE A LATE FILED BRIEF. CAL. RULES OF COURT, RULE 3.1300; KAPITANSKI V. VON'S GROCERY CO., INC. (1983) 146 CA3D 29, 32-33. NONETHELESS, THE COURT HAS REVIEWED AND CONSIDERED THE OPPOSITION AND ITS EXHIBITS. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO PROPERLY ANSWER DISCOVERY AND HIS OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION WERE WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. SANCTIONS ARE GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,560 AGAINST PLAINTIFF STEVE RYAN, PAYABLE TO EDWARD HIGGINBOTHAM WITHIN 30 DAYS OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL SHALL SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: CHARLES GEERHART; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

NOTICE OF MOTION, AND MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, IN SUPPORT OF A SECOND MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES; AND FOR TERMINATING AND MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1873.00 (TRANSACTION ID # 16225002) FILED BY DEFENDANT KELLEY, IAN HEARING SET FOR SEP-07-2016 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745. (D302)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Jul-08-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Steve Ryan, in pro per, Tel. 415.308.7653, for plaintiffs, (arrived on time, but was not present in the courtroom when the case was called);
Ed Higginbotham, Attorney at Law, Tel. 415.581.0885, for defendants.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. DEFENDANT IAN KELLEY Motion To Compel Discovery Responses is granted. The Court finds that the responses served by plaintiff Ryan on May 31 were devoid of substance, and the blanket objections included in those responses were frivolous and improper, and are hence overruled. Plaintiff shall serve objection-free responses to all discovery requests that were the subject of this motion within 20 days of service of this order. The Opposition brief was filed on June 28, which is late under CCP sec. 1005 (nine court days required). The Court may ignore a late filed brief. Cal. Rules of Court, Rule 3.1300; Kapitanski v. Von's Grocery Co., Inc. (1983) 146 CA3d 29, 32-33. Nonetheless, the Court has reviewed and considered the Opposition and its exhibits. The Court finds that plaintiff's failure to properly answer discovery and his opposition to this motion were without substantial justification. Sanctions are granted in the amount of $2,560 against plaintiff Steve Ryan, payable to Edward Higginbotham within 30 days of service of this order. Counsel shall submit a proposed order. Judge Pro Tem: Charles Geerhart; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Continued To Jun-15-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT IAN KELLEY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause and second causes of action for legal malpractice and unjust enrichment and overruled as to the third cause of action for fraud. While the legal malpractice claim is not uncertain, consideration of the judicially noticeable motion for reconsideration filed by plaintiff Steve Ryan in the underlying action discloses that it may be time-barred per Code of Civil Procedure 340.6. Mr. Ryan is given leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege facts showing that his legal malpractice claim is not time-barred notwithstanding the motion for reconsideration he filed in the underlying action. Unjust enrichment is not itself a cognizable claim for damages. Mr. Ryan is given leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege a recognized claim under California law that he is entitled to recover some or all of the money he paid Mr. Kelly. Mr. Ryan's fraud claim is adequately pled. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
